methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terra Drone, a Tokyo-listed public company in the Drones & Autonomous sector founded by Toru Tokushige, presents a valuation challenge due to limited comparable data. The Drones & Autonomous sector contains only four publicly tracked peers, none of which are direct same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables. The relevant peer universe consists of five private-stage companies: Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air, and ACSL.

Peer-median valuation methodology is inapplicable here. The narrow peer set lacks the minimum three datapoints needed to establish a defensible median within stage and tier filters. Applying an all-sector median would introduce significant distortion given the heterogeneity of business models, technology maturity, and commercialization timelines within Drones & Autonomous. No other triangulation methods were supported by the available data.

This analysis cannot produce a quantified valuation range. The absence of public comparables, combined with insufficient same-stage private peers meeting filtering criteria, means standard venture valuation approaches do not yield reliable results. A credible valuation for Terra Drone would require either: (1) disclosure of the IPO pricing and implied metrics to establish a public baseline, or (2) acquisition of additional comparable transaction data within the narrowly defined peer segment. The key uncertainty is whether Terra Drone's business model and stage align with the five identified private peers or whether it operates in a distinct subsegment of the sector.
